chunks.text: Deep learning system aids study of marine ecology - EECS at Berkeley The research of postdoctoral scholar Oscar Beijbom (supervised by Prof. Trevor Darrell at the Berkeley Vision and Learning Center ) is being used to help coral researchers speed up the under-sea mapping of reefs around the world. Beijbom’s neural-networking algorithm is part of a software project, featured in a Nature article , that can zip through and classify over a million photographs in a matter of months. Categories Research Students Related Links Nature: Computers on the reef XL Catlin Global Reef Record View Open Faculty Positions
